what_it_does: Defines a React SourcePanel and pure helper functions that turn a Territory API record into a human-readable 'full record' view: extracting plain-text content, counting inbound references, filtering operator comment notes, and rendering a side panel with label, meaning, loading/error states, fuller content, user notes, and provenance.
observations: The panel is built as an App-level sibling, deliberately outside the V (rhm/) swap-seam container so it survives container replacement.
It consumes an App-side gallery:verb dispatch, matching the navigate re-centre composition contract.
All rendering uses Fresh Paper CSS tokens; no hardcoded colours appear in the component.
The code enforces an 'operator-law': no raw addresses, code, or machine record keys are shown to the user.
contentFrom recursively extracts human text from strings, string arrays (as bullet lines), or a fixed set of object fields, degrading to null for non-plain structures.
SOURCE_MAX caps displayed content at 4000 characters with an honest ellipsis.
territoryNotes only returns comment-type directions with non-empty string values, excluding reactions/favours.
Escape key and scrim click both close the panel, while the V handle keeps its own listeners.
standouts: The 'operator-law' is encoded directly in the extraction and filtering logic, not just comments.
The panel is intentionally decoupled from the V container for swap-seam durability.
It closes the Note verb round-trip by reading operator comments back into the source view.
conventions: Follows React functional component and TypeScript export patterns.
Uses CSS class names prefixed with v- and src- and relies on external Fresh Paper tokens.
Keeps data-transform helpers pure and exported for testability.
Uses semantic ARIA roles and labels on the dialog and close button.
Breaks typical modal semantics by setting aria-modal='false' on a dialog-like overlay with a scrim.
Attaches the Escape listener to the window inside useEffect rather than using React's synthetic event system.
concerns: contentFrom silently drops non-string array items and nested objects, which may hide data the operator would want to see.
The object field traversal order (output, text, digest, summary, body, content) is arbitrary and may select the wrong human text.
aria-modal='false' on a visually modal overlay may confuse assistive technology users.
The addr field in SourceView is typed but the staleness guard using it is not implemented in this file.
SOURCE_MAX truncation uses a regex that can remove the last partial word entirely.
Type assertions remain after filtering, though they are harmless.
notes: A human-centered source inspector that stays outside the V container and enforces a strict no-machine-address UI contract while reading operator notes back into the record view.
questions: Where is SourceView populated and how is the staleness guard using addr enforced?
What is the exact shape of Territory.corpus_content versus Territory.identity in practice?
What is the V (rhm/) container and the gallery:verb dispatch contract precisely?
How are Fresh Paper tokens mapped to the CSS class names used here?
Why is aria-modal set to false for a dialog that behaves modally with a scrim?
Are there other direction types besides comment that should surface as prose notes?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it reveals the fuller human record behind a dot, plus provenance and operator notes, while hiding raw machine identifiers.
apparent_themes: Operator-law: no raw addresses or code shown
Fresh Paper token system with zero hardcoded colour
Honest emptiness rather than raw data dumps
intention_signals: Build a durable, human-centered 'source' inspector that survives container swaps, enforces a strict no-machine-address UI contract, and closes the loop between operator notes and the system record display.
novelty: The explicit encoding of an 'operator-law' in the data extraction logic, and the architectural placement of the panel outside the V swap-seam container so it outlives container replacement.
contribution: A reusable set of pure territory-to-human-view transformers and a panel component that can display a fuller record, operator notes, and inbound reference count while remaining independent of the V container; the human-text extraction heuristic and note-filtering logic are keepable pieces for any fused source-inspector.
summary_for_embedding: SourcePanel.tsx defines a React side-panel and helper functions that convert a Territory record into a human-only source view with capped plain-text content, operator notes, and inbound reference count, kept outside the V container for swap-seam durability.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a durable, App-level 'Source' verb result surface that reveals the fuller human record behind a selected dot while hiding raw identifiers and reading back operator notes.

symbols.does: Type describing the human-facing payload shown in the Source panel.
symbols.description: Defines the SourceView shape: a hidden record address for staleness guarding, a human label, an optional meaning, optional fuller content, an inbound reference count, the operator's own notes, and loading/error flags.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/source/SourcePanel.tsx::readTerritoryContent | function | export function readTerritoryContent(t: Territory): string | null {
symbols.does: Extracts plain-human content from a Territory and caps its length.
symbols.description: Prefers corpus_content over identity, runs contentFrom recursively, and truncates the result to SOURCE_MAX with an ellipsis; returns null when no plain text is found.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/source/SourcePanel.tsx::contentFrom | function | function contentFrom(cand: unknown): string | null {
symbols.does: Recursively extracts human-readable text from arbitrary territory content.
symbols.description: Returns strings directly, renders string-array items as bullet lines, and checks a fixed field order on objects; any non-plain or deeply nested structure degrades to null to avoid raw dumps.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/source/SourcePanel.tsx::territoryRefCount | function | export function territoryRefCount(t: Territory): number {
symbols.does: Counts inbound board references for provenance.
symbols.description: Reads relations.edges_in length when present and returns 0 otherwise, letting the UI omit the provenance line when there are no authored links.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/source/SourcePanel.tsx::territoryNotes | function | export function territoryNotes(t: Territory): string[] {
symbols.does: Extracts operator-authored comment notes from directions.
symbols.description: Filters directions of type 'comment' with non-empty string values, excluding reactions and favours, so text left via the Note verb reads back in the source panel.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/source/SourcePanel.tsx::SourcePanel | component | export function SourcePanel({ source, onClose }: { source: SourceView | null; onClose: () => void }) {
symbols.does: React component that renders the full-record side panel.
symbols.description: Conditionally renders loading, error, fuller content, operator notes, and inbound reference count; attaches Escape and scrim close handlers; uses Fresh Paper CSS classes with no hardcoded colours.

symbols.does: Local Escape key handler inside SourcePanel.
symbols.description: Listens for the Escape key on window while a source is open and calls onClose; registered and cleaned up in a useEffect tied to the source prop.
